faqs.a: It's a form of psychotherapy where two partners meet with a therapist to work on their relationship, with the relationship itself as the client rather than either person alone. The therapist helps the couple see their patterns and build better ways of relating.

faqs.q: Does the therapist decide who is right in couples therapy?
faqs.a: No. Couples therapy isn't a place where the therapist decides who's right or tells a couple whether to stay together. That choice stays with the partners.

faqs.q: Is couples therapy only for relationships in crisis?
faqs.a: No. It isn't only a last resort before a breakup. Many couples use it to get ahead of problems or to grow, not just to repair serious damage.
